expectations.text: Agent requires explicit rollback condition to be agreed with user before starting
expectations.evidence: "Big Bang requires an explicit rollback plan agreed with the user before starting — what condition triggers rollback, who decides, and is the rollback path tested?" and "This is not optional. It is a hard gate in the skill. Without it, Phase 2 cannot begin."

expectations.text: Agent asks who decides to trigger a rollback
expectations.evidence: Question 2 under STOP section: "Decision authority: Who decides to pull the rollback trigger? The team lead? A specific engineer? A group vote?"

expectations.text: 40+ call sites are flagged as a specific risk factor that makes big bang harder to verify
expectations.evidence: The response mentions 40+ call sites multiple times (e.g., "40+ direct FirebaseAnalytics.getInstance() usages", "40+ files means this list is generated before any code is touched") but never explicitly frames 40+ call sites as a risk factor that makes big bang harder to verify. The risk framing appears only in the parallel strategy section, where 40+ sites is cited as a reason to prefer the parallel approach over in-place — not as a reason big bang is harder to verify.

expectations.text: Agent does NOT start the actual migration code — stops to establish the rollback agreement first
expectations.evidence: The response ends at Phase 1 discovery with a STOP gate: "Phase 2 does not start until: Rollback plan agreed (trigger condition + decision authority + rollback path tested)" and no migration code is written.
